article_title: All Five DNA and RNA Building Blocks Found in Asteroid Ryugu Samples, Strengthening the Case That Life's Ingredients Came From Space

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: All Five DNA and RNA Building Blocks Found in Asteroid Ryugu Samples, Strengthening the Case That Life's Ingredients Came From Space
content_preview.summary: Scientists have detected all five canonical nucleobases in pristine samples from asteroid Ryugu, the first confirmation of a complete set of DNA and RNA building blocks in material collected directly from space.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
Scientists have identified all five nucleobases essential for DNA and RNA in samples collected directly from asteroid Ryugu, providing the clearest evidence yet that the molecular foundations of life are produced in space and may have been delivered to Earth billions of years ago by asteroid impacts.

The study, [published in Nature Astronomy on March 17](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41550-026-02791-z), was led by Toshiki Koga of Japan's Agency for Marine-Earth Science and Technology (JAMSTE...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent science reporting with clear explanation of significance, proper caveats, and good comparative context (Murchison, Orgueil, Bennu).
editor_notes.source_verification: Space.com (fetched): Confirms all 5 nucleobases, Hayabusa2, December 2020, 20 mg, 900m asteroid, two sites, 300M km, Nature Astronomy March 17, Late Heavy Bombardment. Phys.org (fetched): Confirms Toshiki Koga, all 5 bases, mass spectrometry, ammonia-nucleobase pathway, pyrimidine/purine ratios, structural isomers, Murchison/Orgueil/Bennu comparison. Nature Astronomy: Not directly fetchable but confirmed by both secondary sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. The ammonia-nucleobase pathway is confirmed as 'previously unrecognized' per Koga's quote in Phys.org. Proper caveat about nucleobases ≠ life confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, measured, scientifically appropriate. No sensationalism despite the exciting finding.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of Ryugu nucleobases. Unique topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality astrobiology news with thorough verification. Ready for publication.
